title: Fee Proposal for Remedial Engineering Services
intro: Thank you for inviting XNDR Consulting to provide this proposal for professional services for the investigative engineering and remedial condition assessment of the above project.

In preparing our proposal we have reviewed the quote request, undertaken a desktop study of the building, and considered the most efficient way of providing services based on our experience with similar projects.
scopeSummary: Scope of Works / specification for the replacement of all balcony balustrades.
DBPA documentation to replace waterproofing on all balconies.

stages.name: Stage 1 — Inspection & Scope of Works
description: Prepare a detailed Scope of Work and specification that aligns with the NSW Government's Design & Building Practitioners Act 2020. Prior to construction the design will encompass elements requiring a Regulated Design Declaration from a Structural and Façade Design Practitioner.
deliverables: Site inspection (measure up) of all affected balconies
Detailed Scope of Work and tender documentation
Scale drawings — elevations, sections and details

items.description: Inspection, Scope of Work and tender documentation (incl. DBPA)

stages.name: Stage 2 — Tender Process
description: Issue the tender package to three (3) contractors, attend an on-site tender meeting, answer queries, then receive submissions and prepare a tender summary with preferred-contractor recommendations.

items.description: Changes to scope following tender (if required)

stages.name: Stage 3 — Preparation of Draft Contracts
description: Provide a draft contract for the Owners Corporation's consideration and manage execution of contract documents, including special conditions over and above AS4000.

stages.name: Stage 4 — Construction & Contract Administration
description: Following execution of the building works contract, XNDR will carry out technical supervision and the role of Superintendent, including site inspections, progress-claim assessment and final certification. All-inclusive up to Practical Completion.

exclusions: Invasive or destructive investigations, unless otherwise stated
Specialised access systems or assisting labour to provide safe access
Areas considered non-trafficable, inaccessible or unsafe on the day
Fire, mechanical, electrical and hydraulic services — require specialist consultants
BCA / planning compliance and OH&S compliance
notes: Access for inspections to be arranged by the Owners Corporation or Strata Manager.
Work undertaken after Practical Completion is charged at our current hourly rates.

terms: Invoices are issued as progress claims on a monthly basis. Payment is required within 14 days of the date of invoice.
Overdue invoices may incur interest at 13% per annum from the date of invoice.
XNDR Consulting shall provide the consulting engineering services described in this proposal together with such other services as may be agreed in writing.
XNDR shall provide the services with the skill, care and diligence generally exercised by competent members of the engineering profession.
The client shall make available all information and documents necessary for XNDR to carry out the services, and XNDR is entitled to rely on them.
The client shall pay the fee and reimbursable expenses set out in this proposal, plus GST where amounts are not expressed as GST-inclusive.
XNDR may claim payment monthly in arrears; the client must pay within 14 days of invoice without set-off or deduction.
Interest may be payable on overdue amounts at 13% per annum from the date of invoice, plus collection costs.
XNDR's aggregate liability arising out of this agreement is limited to the amount of the fee, or $5,000,000 if no amount is stated.
XNDR is not liable for indirect, consequential or special losses including loss of profit or business opportunity.
Ownership of intellectual property created under this agreement vests in XNDR; the client is granted a licence to use it for the project.
Either party may terminate on the terms set out in this agreement; XNDR may suspend services immediately if the client fails to pay.
Any dispute shall first be negotiated in good faith and, failing resolution, referred to mediation.
Neither party may assign or sublet any obligation under this agreement without the written consent of the other.
